title: Pulp Figma Plugin Export
description: Envelope emitted by the 'Design for Pulp' Figma plugin. Consumed by `pulp import-design --from figma-plugin --file <path>`. See planning/2026-05-28-pulp-figma-plugin-strategy.md §7.2 for the parser mapping to Pulp's DesignIR.

format_version.description: Export format identity. Bumped on incompatible JSON-shape changes.

parser_version.description: Plugin emitter version. Bumped independently as the plugin gains coverage.

compat_schema_version.description: Pulp compat.json schema version this export was generated against.

version.description: Plugin version that produced this export.

source_uri.description: URN of the Figma file + node, e.g. figma://<fileKey>/<nodeId>

library_manifest.description: Pulp Figma Library version metadata at time of export. Plugin reads tools/figma-plugin/library-manifest.json and copies the active section here. Emitted as null when no library snapshot is available (serialize.ts: ctx.libraryManifest ?? null).

additionalProperties.description: Figma ComponentSetNode.key for each Pulp library widget

tokens.description: Design tokens extracted from Figma variables. Maps to IRTokens in Pulp.

asset_manifest.description: Maps to IRAssetManifest after Phase 4 adds asset_manifest to DesignIR.

diagnostics.description: Maps to ImportDiagnostic[]. Plugin records unsupported features here so the importer can surface them without re-running detection.

font_family_assets.description: Deduplicated catalogue of every font (family + style + weight + italic) referenced by text nodes in the export. Drives Pulp's runtime font resolution: the consumer (Skia SkFontMgr) looks up each entry against system fonts, falls back to Pulp's bundled OFL set (Inter / Roboto / etc.), and emits a `recognition_unavailable` diagnostic when neither resolves. Note: the Figma plugin API intentionally does NOT expose font binaries, so `asset_id` stays unset for plain captures; it's populated only when the plugin's drag-drop escape hatch (issue follow-up) has carried a user-supplied TTF/OTF in `asset_manifest.assets`.

node.description: An IR node as emitted by serialize.ts::toEnvelopeNode. Semantic audio-widget data lives at the NODE ROOT (audio_widget/label/min/max/default + attributes.binding), NOT in nested audio/binding sub-objects. The C++ parser (core/view/src/design_ir_json.cpp::parse_ir_node) reads these root-level fields directly.

type.description: Node kind. Pulp library widgets get specific kinds (knob, fader, meter, ...). Everything else is one of the generic types.

figma_node_id.description: Native Figma node id. Populates IRNode.source_node_id after parsing.

content.description: Text content (when type=text). Maps to IRNode.text_content.

audio_widget.description: Recognised Pulp audio-widget kind. Emitted at the node root when serialize.ts sees node.library_widget_kind. The C++ parser maps this onto IRNode.audio_widget (enum). Equal to figma.library_widget_kind when present.

label.description: Audio-widget label. Root-level; maps to IRNode.audio_label.

min.description: Audio-widget minimum value. Root-level; maps to IRNode.audio_min.

max.description: Audio-widget maximum value. Root-level; maps to IRNode.audio_max.

default.description: Audio-widget default value. Root-level; maps to IRNode.audio_default.

attributes.description: Free-form string passthrough map emitted at the node root by serialize.ts. Each entry becomes an IRNode.attributes[*] entry in the C++ parser. Emitted only when at least one attribute is present.

binding.description: Audio param / event / theme binding identifier (from node.audio_binding). Consumed by the binding resolver (bindParam JS helper + `pulp bindings check`).

units.description: Display units for the audio-widget value (from node.audio_units).

style.description: Snake_case keys. Parser maps to IRStyle (which uses the same snake_case in C++).

layout.description: Maps to IRLayout. Padding is nested as an object here; parser flattens to padding_top/right/bottom/left.

font_family_asset.description: One row in the deduplicated font catalogue carried on `font_family_assets`. Populated by extractScene().collectFontFamilyAssets — every (family, style, weight, italic) tuple referenced by a text node appears exactly once, in first-encounter order.

style.description: Figma style string — verbatim from TextNode.fontName.style ("Regular", "Semi Bold", "Italic", "Bold Italic", etc.).

weight.description: Numeric font-weight when figma exposes it on the text node (TextNode.fontWeight). Omitted when not numeric (mixed weight).

italic.description: True when the style string indicates italic (case-insensitive substring match on "italic"). Lets the runtime resolve italic variants without re-parsing the style string.

asset_id.description: Reference into asset_manifest.assets. Set only when the user has supplied a TTF/OTF via the plugin's drag-drop escape hatch (follow-up). For plain captures this is absent; the runtime falls back to system-font lookup.

figma_metadata.description: Figma-specific identity / provenance, packed into the node's `figma` sub-object by serialize.ts. The C++ parser reads figma.library_widget_kind / figma.component_key as needed. The six positional fields below are always present; the component/library fields are emitted only when the node is an instance / recognised library widget.

absolute_transform.description: Node.relative_transform copied through as a 2x3 affine matrix.

library_widget_kind.description: Recognised Pulp widget kind, mirrors the node-root audio_widget field.

asset_id.description: Stable handle used by nodes via asset_ref.

local_path.description: Path relative to the JSON file.

path.description: Pointer into the JSON tree, e.g. /root/children/4
